cues.label: The structure
cues.headline: A parent forest
cues.narration: Every node points toward a root; two nodes share a group exactly when they share a root.

cues.label: Real union
cues.headline: Different roots merge
cues.narration: When an edge's endpoints have different roots, attach one under the other and drop the count by one.

cues.label: Redundant edge
cues.headline: Same root is a cycle
cues.narration: If both endpoints already share a root, the edge joins nothing new — a redundant edge, a cycle — so nothing changes.

cues.label: Optimizations
cues.headline: Compression + rank
cues.narration: Path compression flattens the chain during find and union by rank keeps trees shallow — near-constant per op.

cues.label: The answer
cues.headline: 2 provinces survive
cues.narration: After all edges, two roots remain — the groups {0,1,2} and {3,4} — so the answer is two provinces.

cues.label: Not a path
cues.headline: Connectivity, not routes
cues.narration: Union Find answers whether two nodes are connected, not the actual path between them — reach for BFS/DFS for that.
